description: All rough insulation installed, air-sealed, and inspected by Baldwin County before drywall hang begins.

items.label: Baldwin County rough framing inspection passed and inspection card signed before insulation crew mobilizes
All MEP rough-in inspections passed (electrical, plumbing, HVAC) — no open walls pending re-inspection
All penetrations through top and bottom plates (pipes, conduit, wire bundles) fire-blocked and air-sealed with approved caulk or foam prior to insulation
Recessed can lights confirmed IC-rated and AT-rated (airtight) where insulation will be in contact; non-IC cans have 3-inch clearance maintained
Exterior sheathing, housewrap, and window/door flashing fully complete and superintendent-verified before cavity insulation begins
HVAC duct leakage test (Duct Blaster or equivalent) completed and results on file — total leakage to outside ≤ 4 CFM25 per 100 sq ft of conditioned floor area per IECC 2021 R403.3.4
Insulation type, R-value, and manufacturer confirmed against approved energy compliance report (Manual J / REScheck) for each assembly: exterior walls, ceiling, floor, rim joists
Spray foam (closed-cell or open-cell) product data sheets on-site; installer's state license number recorded in job file

Batt insulation (if used) confirmed unfaced or kraft-faced as required per assembly location — no kraft-faced batts against conditioned space side in hot-humid climate zones without vapor retarder review
Rigid foam board (if used) verified for correct thickness, R-value, and fire-rated exposure limitation per ICC and manufacturer specs
Sufficient material quantities staged on-site to complete full scope without mid-job delays; delivery receipt logged in Cost Tracker
All top-plate-to-ceiling drywall plane transitions sealed with code-compliant sealant or spray foam before attic insulation installed
Rim joist bays fully air-sealed and insulated (minimum R-15 closed-cell spray foam or rigid foam plus sealant at all edges) per IECC 2021 R402.4
Attic hatch (pull-down stair or access panel) has minimum R-38 insulated cover installed and weatherstripped
Electrical boxes on exterior walls fitted with foam gaskets or sealed with putty pads; superintendent spot-checked minimum 10% of all exterior-wall boxes
Cantilever floors and tray ceiling transitions air-sealed at all framing interfaces before insulation cover
Blower door pre-drywall test completed; result documented — target ≤ 3.0 ACH50 for Palmetto quality standard (IECC 2021 code max is 5.0 ACH50 for Climate Zone 2)
Exterior wall cavity insulation fills full depth of stud bay with no voids, compressions, or gaps; superintendent inspected all exterior walls and photographed representative bays on each elevation
Batt insulation (where applicable) split and fitted around wiring and blocking without compression — no 'picture framing' or folded edges reducing effective R-value
Cathedral ceiling or conditioned attic assembly maintains required ventilation channel (minimum 1-inch airspace) between insulation and roof deck where vented assembly is specified
Spray foam lift thicknesses comply with manufacturer's maximum-per-pass specification; no scorching, delamination, or shrinkage visible; off-ratio foam flagged and removed before sign-off
Crawl space or under-floor insulation (if applicable) installed with insulation facing correct direction; wire hangers or supports at maximum 24-inch spacing — no sagging batts
Garage-to-living-space common walls and ceiling insulated to full exterior-wall R-value and air-sealed per IRC R302.6 fire separation requirements
Insulation certificate (RESNET / manufacturer's product label) attached to electrical panel or posted in prominent location per IECC 2021 R401.3
Installed R-values confirmed against Alabama IECC Climate Zone 2 minimums: walls R-13+5ci or R-20, ceilings R-38 minimum, floors R-13 minimum
Vapor retarder class verified for each assembly per IECC 2021 R702.7 — Class III (latex paint) acceptable for Climate Zone 2 wall assemblies; no poly sheeting on interior of exterior walls
Insulation contractor's certificate of installation (signed, with stated R-values by location) collected and uploaded to project Drive folder
Baldwin County insulation inspection scheduled and inspection card updated upon pass
Superintendent final walk completed with insulation foreman — all deficiencies noted on punch list and corrected before sign-off
Photo documentation of all exterior wall cavities, rim joists, attic plane, and mechanical chases uploaded to project folder and time-stamped
Baldwin County insulation inspection card stamped and passed — physical card on-site and photo copy in Drive
Project manager confirms no open change orders affecting wall assemblies, window sizes, or structural members that would require re-work behind drywall
Drywall sub notified of hang start date; site confirmed clear of insulation debris, excess materials staged or removed
